def: s.m. Glory, splendour, lustre; fame, renown, distinction, celebrity, name, reputation, character; praise, eulogium; — luck (see the pop. form jas)
subs: s.m. Good and bad reputation, fame and infamy; praise and dispraise
or yaś-pat, or jas-pat, adj. & s.m. (f. -patnī), Famous, excellent, renowned, reputable; — a famous man, a celebrity
s.m. An encomiast, a panegyrist, eulogist
adj. & s.m. Honour-loving; desirous or eager for fame; ambitious; — one who is eager for fame, an ambitious man
adj. Causing renown, conferring fame or distinction, rendering famous or glorious; famous, glorious

r: yāsamin, yāsmin, yāsaman, yāsimīn, yāsmīn, yāsamūn, yāsamīn
def: s.m. Jasmine
subs: (or -bū), Scented like jasmine; redolent of jasmine.

def: s.m. Plenty, affluence, opulence; — ease; — adj. & s.m. Left (hand, &c.); — the left side; — an unlucky person
more: (from يسر Heb. and ) s. m. 1. Left, the left side. 2. Plenty, opulence, affluence. يسارويمين yasār o yamīn, Right and left. دست يسار dast-i-yasār, The left hand.

ety: changeable in comp. to yaśah̥, or yaśo
def: s.m. Glory, splendour; fame, &c. (see the contrac. form yaś)
subs: s.f. (lit. 'glory-giving'), Name of the wife of the cowherd Nand, and the foster-mother of Krishṇ
adj. & s.m. See the contrac. form yaś-kām, s.v. yaś
s.f. = yaśo-dā, q.v.; — s.f. The third lunar night
yaśas-vān, or yaśas-want, adj. (f. -vatī), Possessing honour or glory, full of honour, honourable; glorious, famous (syn. yaśasvī).

def: adj. = yaśas-kar = yaś-kar, q.v.; — 2˚ adj. & s.m. (f. -ī), Of small or little repute; — one of little repute; — 3˚ s.m. = yaśas = yaś, q.v.
adj. & s.m. (f. -vinī), Possessing honour or glory, honourable, glorious, famous, renowned, celebrated; — a famous man, a celebrity, &c.
s.m. Horsemen attendant upon a man of rank; — a state-messenger; an officer of parade, the mace-bearer who goes before carrying the wand of state; a pursuivant; a captain of the guard.
more: s. m. An officer of parade, a pursuivant, a captain of the guard.

def: s.m. Jasper; calcedony; agate.
s.m. Jasper, &c. (i.q. yashb, q.v.). s.m. Jasper; calcedony; agate.
adj. Of jasper, jasper.

s.f. (rarely m.), or यष्टी yashṭī, s.f. A staff, stick, wand; a staff armed with iron, &c. (used as a weapon); a mace, a club (see the pop. lāṭhī); a pole; a flagstaff; — a perch; stem; support; a palisade; — the arm and fore-arm; — a string, thread; a string of pearls; a necklace; — liquorice; — the plant Clerodendrum siphonanthus; any creeping plant
subs: s.m. Liquorice, or the root of the Abrus precatorius (which is used instead of it).

def: s.m. (f. -ā), The lapwing.
s.f. A staff, stick, &c. (i.q. yashṭi); — a necklace; — stick-liquorice; — a pond.
adj. = yaśasvī.

r: yāsimīn, yāsmīn, yāsamin, yāsmin, yāsaman, yāsamūn, yāsamīn
def: s.m. Jasmine
subs: (or -bū), Scented like jasmine; redolent of jasmine.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian یَاسَمِین (yāsamīn).

def: adj. (dakh.) Motherless ( Harris ).
s.f. corr. of yaśo-dā, q.v.
s.f. = S يشومتي यशोमती yaśo-matī, s.f. See s.v. yaśas.
s.f. = S يشودا यशोदा yaśo-dā, s.f. See s.v. yaśas.

ety: inf. n. of يسر 'to be easy,' &c.
def: s.m. Ease; — prosperity; opulence, wealth; — facility, lenity, softness
more: (Heb. or ) 1. s. m. Facility, lenity. 2. Prosperity, wealth. ¶ yasar, adj. 1. Easy, soft, gentle, humane. 2. Ready, prepared.
subs: adj. Easy, soft, mild, gentle, humane; — ready, prepared.

def: s.m.(?) Name of the sacred city of Medina, in Arabia.
adj. = yaśasvī, q.v. adj. & s.m. (f. -vinī), Possessing honour or glory, honourable, glorious, famous, renowned, celebrated; — a famous man, a celebrity, &c.
s.m. The king of the bees; — the male bee; — ʻthe queen-bee.'
more: s. m. The queen ( lit. king) bee.

def: 1˚ corr. of aśuddh, q.v.; — 2˚ = yah aśuddh.
s.f. corr. of yaśo-matī, q.v.s.v. yaśas.
s.f. corr. of yaśumatī, q.v.
adj. corr. of yaśasvī, q.v. adj. & s.m. (f. -vinī), Possessing honour or glory, honourable, glorious, famous, renowned, celebrated; — a famous man, a celebrity, &c.
part. Restrained, held in, curbed, kept in check, governed, &c.; limited, moderate, temperate
subs: adj. & s.m. (f. -ā), Observing vows; keeping to engagements; holding to any promised observance; — one who observes a vow; one who holds to an engagement, &c.
(˚ta+in˚), adj. & s.m. Having the organs of sense restrained; of subdued passions; chaste, pure; — one who has subdued his senses, &c. (syn. jitendrī).
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian ـِیَّت (-iyyat), in turn from Arabic ـِيَّة (-iyya). Compare Hindi -ता (-tā) and Hindi -त्व (-tva).

def: s.f. Recompense, requital, retaliation, revenge; — acute pain, agony, torment, torture; punishment inflicted by Yam, the torments of hell.
more: s. f. Pain, agony, sharp or acute pain.

def: part. adj. To be exerted; to be persevered in, to be striven after.
s.m. Ante-chamber; hall of audience.
